text: Did you know lightning creates nitrogen? When lightning strikes, it generates extreme heat, breaking nitrogen molecules apart. This process forms nitrogen oxides, which then mix with rainwater. Plants absorb this nitrogen, essential for growth. So, lightning helps feed the Earth! Yet, climate change is affecting lightning frequency. Less lightning could mean less nitrogen for crops. Curious about more science secrets? Hit that follow button!
